icpRubric.notes: Each `value` is an independent tag: store and score as a **set** (unordered, dedupe). A single account may match many tags (e.g. public cloud + warehouse + streaming). Pattern-based—orthogonal to `firmographics.business_model`. Sparse stacks may use only `minimal_or_outsourced`.

description: Multi-tag set of observable data and infrastructure patterns (deployment, analytics, integration, systems of record, scale, ML, edge, compliance). Pick all that apply; order does not matter. Describes stack posture, not revenue model.

values.label: Public cloud primary
values.definition: Core workloads run on public cloud (e.g. AWS, GCP, Azure) with standard shared-responsibility operations.

values.label: Hybrid / private cloud
values.definition: Significant split between on-premises and cloud, or reliance on private cloud / dedicated estates rather than default public multi-tenant SaaS only.

values.label: On-prem / self-hosted core
values.definition: Primary systems run in owned data centers or fully customer-operated environments; cloud may be peripheral.

values.label: Isolated, sovereign, or air-gapped
values.definition: Strong network isolation, sovereignty, classified, or air-gap constraints driving where data and services may run.

values.label: Analytics warehouse + BI
values.definition: Central cloud or on-prem warehouse/large-scale SQL analytics plus BI layer (dashboards, semantic models, governed metrics).

values.label: Data lake / lakehouse
values.definition: Lake or lakehouse pattern with raw and curated zones, often unified query across object storage and structured layers.

values.label: OLTP / operational systems primary
values.definition: Transactional databases and operational apps dominate; no mature enterprise analytics platform (or analytics is ad hoc).

values.label: Event streaming & real-time
values.definition: Message buses, stream processing, or low-latency pipelines are first-class (not only nightly batch).

values.label: ETL/ELT & integration hub
values.definition: Batch or micro-batch pipelines, managed ELT, or iPaaS as the main way systems stay in sync.

values.label: CRM-centered hub
values.definition: CRM is the primary commercial customer object system; much reporting and workflow orbit sales/marketing data.

values.label: ERP / finance core
values.definition: ERP or finance suite is the backbone for entities, orders, inventory, manufacturing, or financial truth.

values.label: High-volume digital telemetry
values.definition: Very large-scale product, web, app, ad, or device event ingestion and processing (common in ads, consumer, high-traffic marketplaces).

values.label: ML platform / feature ops
values.definition: Operational ML: feature stores, training/serving split, model registries, or sustained batch/inference pipelines beyond one-off experiments.

values.label: Vector / embeddings (RAG, semantic search)
values.definition: Embedding generation, vector databases, or retrieval-augmented patterns are part of production architecture.

values.label: Edge, IoT, OT
values.definition: Field devices, plants, vehicles, or OT systems integrate into data flows; compute or buffering at the edge matters.

values.label: Regulated / compliance-bound data
values.definition: Data systems explicitly shaped by regimes such as HIPAA, PCI, GDPR-class controls, or validated life-sciences IT practices.

values.label: Minimal stack / outsourced IT
values.definition: Light internal data platform; heavy reliance on MSPs, default SaaS sprawl, spreadsheets, or no dedicated data engineering.
